frameworks.name: transient-trajectory dynamics
frameworks.one_line: Working memory encoded by dynamic transient neural trajectories rather than fixed states.

frameworks.name: attractor dynamics / persistent activity
frameworks.one_line: Classical view that WM relies on stable persistent firing attractors.

frameworks.name: recurrent neural networks
frameworks.one_line: RNN architecture extended with self-inhibition, sparse connectivity, hierarchical topology.

frameworks.name: reinforcement learning
frameworks.one_line: Used as task framework for delayed choice and spatial memory evaluation.

core_question: Does working memory rely on persistent attractor activity or on transient neural trajectories?
key_assumptions: WM mechanism can be probed via biologically plausible RNN architectures
Self-inhibition, sparse connection, and hierarchical topology yield biologically realistic dynamics
Activity patterns in RNNs can be compared to animal recordings
manipulations: Network architecture: TRNN vs vanilla RNN
Task type: delayed choice and spatial memory RL tasks
key_findings: TRNNs produce transient activity resembling animal recordings
TRNNs retain versatility across variable encoding times
TRNNs outperform vanilla RNNs on delayed choice and spatial memory RL tasks
interpretations: Evidence supports transient-activity theory of working memory from a model-design perspective
